title: Hill Cipher Encryption and Decryption Process
description: A flowchart illustrating the steps to encrypt and decrypt a message using the Hill cipher, a classical polygraphic substitution cipher based on linear algebra.
relationships: Plaintext is divided into blocks and converted to numbers
Each block is multiplied by the key matrix modulo 26
Result is converted back to letters to form ciphertext
For decryption, ciphertext is multiplied by the inverse key matrix modulo 26
